text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, you might rename unclear variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate logic. Refactoring does not add new features; it simply improves the quality of existing code. Over time, if code is not refactored, it can accumulate technical debt—problems like confusing structure or slow performance that make future changes harder. By regularly refactoring, developers keep the codebase healthy and reduce the cost of future development.
